source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Two parallel lines $\ell_1$ and $\ell_2$ lie on a plane, distance $d$ apart. On $\ell_1$ there are an infinite number of points $A_1, A_2, A_3, ...$ , in that order, with $A_nA_{n+1} = 2$ for all $n$. On $\ell_2$ there are an infinite number of points $B_1, B_2, B_3,...$ , in that order and in the same direction, satisfying $B_nB_{n+1} = 1$ for all $n$. Given that $A_1B_1$ is perpendicular to both $\ell_1$ and $\ell_2$, express the sum $\sum_{i=1}^{\infty} \angle A_iB_iA_{i+1}$ in terms of $d$.\n<image1>

Given that $A_1B_1$ is perpendicular to both $\ell_1$ and $\ell_2$, express the sum $\sum_{i=1}^{\infty} \angle A_iB_iA_{i+1}$ in terms of $d$.\n<image1>
